channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: 'Operation: Belly of the Beast' Returns to Warframe, Digital Extremes Opens TennoCon Digital & Merch Pack Sales
item.content:encoded:
Warframe brings back Operation: Belly of the Beast and it's time to get your clan to work together and take on threats across the Origin System. There are new and returning rewards to collect, as well as specials for those in clans. Digital Extremes has also just put TennoCon 2026 digital and merch packs on sale.

The clan event runs through June 1st at 2pm ET, and if you’ve completed the Jade Shadows quest and The New War, then you’re ready. Join up with your clan to get started, and take on the Jade Light Eximus units that spawn instead of regular Eximus units. Help Ordis to stop Parvis via one of three options. As you continue in the associated missions, you’ll access nodes that let you take on Parvos’ forces.  

This event is also preparation for the upcoming Jade Shadows: Constellations major update. 

Operation: Belly of the Beast  has a new Brutus node on Uranus and that itself comes with a new Operation variant that will have you beating back more Jade Light Eximus units. You can choose to summon a Sister of Parvos for more Volatile Motes to aid you.

There will also be timed Operation alerts across the Origin System. These non-Ascension missions award Volatile Motes. Collect these and a community counter will rise and make progress towards Collective rewards including a new variant of the Jade Light Ephemera.

There’s more to know about the Operation, as well as the long list of rewards, both new and returning.

TennoCon 2026 will return to London, Ontario Canada in July. Digital Extremes has just opened up sales for digital and merch packs. If you have a TennoCon ticket, you’ll get these packs automatically, but for the rest of us, These are ways to get your hands on special contents including the Hunhullus Ephemera, themed emote, glyph, sigil, and display for TennoCon2026, and more. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/operation-belly-of-the-beast-returns-to-warframe-digital-extremes-opens-tennocon-digital-and-merch-pack-sales-2000137983
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Conan Exiles Enahanced Is Live, Bringing Unreal Engine Upgrade, Huge Map Merge And More
item.content:encoded:
The recently-announced Enhanced version of Conan Exiles has gone live, launching an overhaul of the entire game’s systems and graphics just ahead of its eighth anniversary. 

At the heart of this upgrade is an upgrade to Unreal Engine 5, which also brings with it major visual updates and a huge install downsize from 105 GB to 61 GB. Crafting and building get a giant upgrade, allowing players to build with supplies from nearby chests. 

The Isle of Siptah and the Exiled Lands maps get a giant merge to allow for seamless movement between the two. If a map owner owns both, they have the option to have both included. Plus, the UI gets a neat overhaul for clarity and improvement.  

If you’ve made a mod for Conan Exiles, they do say it’s likely compatible, but “will require upgrading, recooking, and repackaging for UE5/Enhanced.” 

The Enhanced patch for Conan Exiles also generally comes with a ton of fixes that make up the bulk of the full patch notes, so if there’s something that’s been bug–ging you for a while (gaps on doors and around buildings, messed-up costumes, glitches and rubber-banding with dead enemies) there’s a chance it got fixed. 

The Unreal Engine 4 version of the game will continue to exist as a “Legacy” version, but there won’t be any further updates for it, and the Bazaar won’t exist there. Characters will have to be moved from the Legacy to Enhanced, or it can be played on private servers. 

Inflexion Studios, the team behind the steampunk fantasy survival game Nightingale, recently shared that it helped with this upgrade after seeing its own game go through a similar upgrade to Unreal Engine 5. 

With the launch, Funcom also dropped a trailer showing off how Conan Exiles Enhanced looks. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/conan-exiles-enahanced-is-live-bringing-unreal-engine-upgrade-huge-map-merge-and-more-2000137982
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: 'The Only Way', Second Update for Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity, is Coming Next Week
item.content:encoded:
The second update for Guild Wars 2: Visions of Eternity, “The Only Way”, is coming next Tuesday, May 12th. The update continues the story, adds a new zone,  two new mastery tiers, new relics, legendary accessories, and more.

“The Only Way” brings us back to the mysterious island of Castora, where you may have set down roots by the beach in a new homestead, but there’s a new threat brewing. The update has five new story chapters, with a new goal of stopping Vloxx and his plans for Tyria. The new chapters will bring us to a new map, Eternity’s Garden. 

The two new mastery tiers will offer account-wide horizontal progression opportunities. There are six new  relics in this update that you can earn and use to customize your builds. There are also new legendary accessories in this update, with one you can earn through PvE content and one through World vs. World. 

The release will also include a Wizard’s Vault refresh with new items and objectives, a raptor mount skin to earn, and more rewards and new content to discover. 

This is the second of the three planned major content updates in the Visions of Eternity expansion cycle. Looking ahead,  the final update will complete the story as well as add more new maps, instance content, more legendary gear, and more.

ANet released a trailer to accompany The Only Way’s announcement, and a “the story so far” video a few days ago. With the update just one week away, we expect to learn some more about what to expect in the coming days.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/the-only-way-second-update-for-guild-wars-2-visions-of-eternity-is-coming-next-week-2000137980
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Conan Exiles Enhanced Preview: A Love Letter to fans
item.content:encoded:
As a fan of Conan, I was heavily involved in Age of Conan, and soon thereafter Conan Exiles. While not as deep into the lore as I might have wanted, it proved to be a phenomenal game to get my Conan fix. My only real complaint was that the Isle of Siptah expansion felt more like a standalone title than an actual expansion. With several substantial updates since, including the addition of the sorcery mechanic, Conan Exiles largely remained the same game it was during its early access period.Until today.

I, like many other fans, was pleasantly surprised to hear that Funcom decided to rebuild the game within Unreal Engine 5, a massive undertaking that completely revamps and reinvigorates the experience to a degree that is virtually unheard of from developers. While there are a few standouts that have lived up to this level of investment, it is nearly unheard of, leaving us to ask, was the technical overhaul worth the effort, and does it live up to the hype?

Hyborian Renaissance: A Free Anniversary Gift 

It's not every day that developers overhaul a nearly decade-old game, let alone invest in a complete engine transition. It's even rarer for these upgrades to be completely free. Yet, that's exactly what Funcom has delivered to celebrate the eighth anniversary of Conan Exiles on Steam. While this is explicitly only for Steam owners, Funcom has stated that they have not completely ruled out a console version in the future. To say the least, this upgrade is an incredible and intimate love letter to the Conan and survival-crafting community!

Visual Fidelity: The Oasis Transformation 

As with any upgrade to Unreal Engine 5, the first thing I noticed in Conan Exiles Enhanced was the increase in graphical fidelity that was offered up on a silver platter. When I first stepped into the desert sands of the Exiled Lands, it was a scene of familiarity; this was what it looked like in 2018 in my mind. It wasn't until reaching the oasis just north of the starting zone that the true depth of this overhaul hit me with the weight of a landslide. 

While the monotonous sea of sands offered little opportunity for standout detail, the lush greenery and water physics of the oasis made this upgrade shine brighter than the desert sun. No longer are the trees faded and washed out, muddy and flat; they possess volumetric depth and fidelity that make the environment feel like real, breathing foliage. The most striking details, however, were the transition from the flat, barely moving or even shimmering water of the original release to the lustrous waves full of realistic caustics and bubbling ripples. This transformation created a night-and-day difference that completely changes the area's atmosphere.

Unifying the Exiled Lands and Siptah 

The changes don't stop there, however. As one might expect from a major engine change, there is more under the hood than just a fresh coat of paint. For starters, both maps are now seamlessly accessible without the need to restart and manage separate saves.

Previously, experiencing Isle of Siptah meant locking yourself out of your established character, starting over fresh again. Instead of feeling like a natural extension of the experience, the expansion felt dislocated from the base game, as if it were its own standalone game. With this new enhanced edition, traveling between the two maps is as simple as a single interaction. Whether you're playing co-op, on a dedicated server, or even singleplayer, you can now transition between maps freely without losing progression on either. This change brings the expansion home, integrating it with the base game to make the entire experience finally feel whole.

Performance, Storage, and Handheld Play 

Performance and stability have long been issues for many in the community. While not the most graphically demanding title, Conan Exiles has suffered from a persistent memory leak since its early days. It is often plagued by frequent stuttering and crashes, which are only exacerbated by high-density player builds. While I was unable to test these high-density areas during my review period, the Enhanced update runs incredibly smoothly on my PC (Ryzen 5900X, RTX 3080, 32 GB RAM) at ultra settings. I maintained a stable 60 FPS, though pushing much beyond that proved challenging. I am happy to report that during my playtime, I experienced zero hiccups or crashes, a massive improvement over the original version. 

The real test will be post-launch, when the masses get their hands on the update. High-density builds have historically been where performance breaks down; it remains to be seen whether this upgrade resolves these long-standing issues.

One specific friction point was completely obliterated with this update: the storage footprint. Conan Exiles previously consumed over 108 GB of drive space. What is surprising is that the Unreal Engine 5 upgrade nearly halves the client size, taking it down to just 58 GB at the time of writing (though this may fluctuate slightly with patching). This reduction is a massive blessing for users with limited or expensive storage.

Steam Deck users will be happy to know that with this upgrade, Conan Exiles receives full, native support. While playable previously, getting it to run often required tweaking community layouts and dealing with performance instability. Now, the control scheme fits like a glove, and the game runs right out of the box. The only constraint is the hardware's thermal ceiling, which naturally caps performance at medium settings with some tweaks on the original Steam Deck. It is a minor trade-off, considering the ability to jump seamlessly between your PC and handheld device.

Old Janks, New UI 

Some may look at this upgrade and wonder: is the jankiness gone? While the engine has been upgraded, the core mechanics and physics remain largely untouched, and honestly, that's for the best. What makes Conan Exiles stand out from other games in the genre is precisely that touch of jankness, it's uniquely "Funcom," a design fingerprint familiar even in the most recent title: Dune: Awakening.

The last major enhancement with this update is the complete interface redesign. Every aspect of the UI, from the main menu down to the map screen, has been completely reworked. No element was left untouched, transforming the bland and utilitarian design of the original release into a sleek, modern experience that complements the visual upgrades perfectly.

Conan Exiles has been a favorite of mine since its early access days. I love the Conan IP and, like many others, was eager for a proper return to the series after the decline of Age of Conan. While my 200+ hours may be a pittance compared to some in this community, I find myself continually pulled back, a true testament to the game's addictive loop and Funcom's dedication. I didn't have an Unreal Engine 5 Conan Exiles upgrade on my 2026 bingo card, but I'm incredibly glad it's here. This anniversary update serves as a genuine love letter to the community, and it's a benchmark that I hope Funcom continues to build upon.

Christopher Saxon

Since joining MMORPG.com as a freelance writer in 2015, Chris has shared his passion for JRPGs and MMORPGs with readers. When he's not gaming, he's often found enjoying anime or spending time with friends and family.



https://www.mmorpg.com/previews/conan-exiles-enhanced-preview-a-love-letter-to-fans-2000137981
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
